short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $2.65B in FY2026 Q1, +30.1% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $2.45B (-7.5% vs current Q) — set by 22 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $0.95.
Next earnings: 2026-07-29 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $11B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $10B → FY2 $12B (+19.8% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 15.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
7-quarter run-rate CAGR: +22.6% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Industrials): Reshoring + defense supercycle drives a multi-year backlog conversion story.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 98.7% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 2.55 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±17.1% ($261.80 – $369.54) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -3.45pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $215.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $300 (5% OTM). High IV (99%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 66.6% across 8 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 8 dates spanning 93–611 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±34.4% ($207.01 – $424.33).
212-day implied move: ±50.8% ($155.16 – $476.18) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $160 (-49.3% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-32.1pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $284 put / short $347 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
